calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-15-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for (administrator) Thomas Lucas.

Mr. Senick reported that we are here regarding the statutory and extraordinary fees, that the probate examiner recommends that the value of the auto not be included as it was turned back in to the dealer, that there is no dispute as to the value, that the administrator spent time dealing with the car, that the beneficiary had wanted the car but could not afford the payments, that it was an asset and statutory fees are granted based on value, that there is no provision in the law to deduct for amount due, that the administrator had to go to the residence and take the car from the roommate who had been using it and that the administrator stored the car in his garage and paid the insurance on it. Mr. Senick further stated that in regards to the extraordinary fee request he understands the court's need to be discretionary but feels it does not have the right to diminish the extraordinary fees to zero, that there was additional work done, that it benefitted the beneficiary, that it required attorney work, that there were many discussions with the decedent's mother regarding personal property that belonged to her son, that they had to convince the roommate that certain items belonged to the decedent and that it seems unfair to ask an attorney to do extra work and then not compensate. The Court cited 1062(d) regarding calculations of gains or losses on sale or other dispositions and took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Declaration Re Father'S D.O.B. Or Social Security Number.
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-26-2014 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Continued To Aug-26-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Senick for Thomas Lucas.

Mr. Senick stated that there was not much further information regarding the Decedent's Father, that his Assistant went on the Social Security data base and found a William T. Cox who was born in 1942, and that he can not say that it is the Father. Mr. Senick also spoke of information that was provided by the Decedent's Mother, stated that there is real property and a car, that someone is living in the property, that the mortgage is not being paid, that the car is being driven and needs to be secured, and that the Mother lives in Georgia. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to August 26, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Senick to file a declaration indicating what he was able to find regarding Social Security records for William T. Cox.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
